summary: High‑energy second set with scorching Scarlet>Fire and epic Morning Dew
blurb: The concert moves from a modest first set into a blistering second set highlighted by Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain and a monster Not Fade Away > Morning Dew. Jerry’s vocals and guitar are fiery, Phil’s bass drives the band, and Brent adds solid B3 work throughout. The best soundboard tape captures the energy with crisp mix despite minor glitches.
review: On November 8, 1979 at the Capital Centre the Dead delivered a show that starts off a bit uneven but erupts into pure kinetic joy in the second set. After a serviceable first half – New Minglewood Blues, Stagger Lee and a segue‑filled run through China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider – the band launches into a scorching Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain that many reviewers call “scorching” and “high‑energy.” From there the groove only intensifies: Lost Sailor > Saint of Circumstance rides a tight drums/space bridge into a thunderous Not Fade Away > Morning Dew, where Jerry’s reggae‑tinged ad‑libs (“I guess it doesn’t matter”) and Phil’s bombastic bass “fatties” lift the crowd to massive chills. Brent’s early B3 work on Dew and his synth touches at the start of Looks Like Rain add texture, while Mickey and Bill provide a rock‑solid rhythmic foundation that drives the jams forward. The best‑rated soundboard capture (gd79-11-08.sbd.litfin.18126.sbeok.shnf) offers a crisp, balanced mix that lets every instrument shine, making this show a must‑listen for anyone craving that late‑’70s Dead firepower.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Three recordings describe the performance as positive and two are mixed, with consistent praise for the second‑set energy, Scarlet>Fire, Not Fade Away > Morning Dew, and strong band interplay. No recurring negative comments about playing were found, so a solid positive adjustment is applied.

best_recording.reason: Superb soundboard capture with crisp, balanced mix; minimal technical issues and the most complete representation of the high‑energy second set.

key_highlights: Scorching Scarlet Begonias > Fire on the Mountain that ignites the second set.
Monumental Not Fade Away > Morning Dew sequence with reggae lilt and massive crowd reaction.
Phil Lesh’s bombastic, booming bass driving the entire performance.
Brent Mydland’s solid B3 work on Dew and overall strong keyboard contributions.

band_performance.Jerry: Passionate vocals and fiery guitar work, especially on the reggae‑tinged Morning Dew and the scorching Scarlet>Fire stretch; his ad‑libs generated huge chills in the crowd.
band_performance.Phil: Bombastic, booming presence with a bass that roared through the set; turned up loud in the mix and provided powerful “fatties” during the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Bob: Added slide work on New Minglewood Blues, contributing subtle texture early in the show.
band_performance.Brent: Delivered solid B3 organ on Morning Dew and synth accents at the start of Looks Like Rain; overall keyboard playing described as strong throughout.
band_performance.Mickey: Anvil‑like drumming with a controlled‑chaos transition into Space and Dew, praised for a solid rhythmic foundation.
band_performance.Billy: Partnered with Mickey to lay down tight drum grooves that propelled the high‑energy jams, earning praise even from listeners not usually focused on drums.
